data.content:
**Yes, C# fully supports `stackalloc`'d `Span` of native-sized integers.** Both `Span<nint>` and `Span<nuint>` work:

```csharp
Span<nint>  a = stackalloc nint[4];   // ✅ works
Span<nuint> b = stackalloc nuint[3];  // ✅ works
```

Test output confirmed correct behavior — indexing, assignment, and arithmetic all work as expected. On this 64-bit system, `nint.Size` is 8 bytes (would be 4 on a 32-bit runtime).

One minor note: `sizeof(nint)` requires an `unsafe` context since the size isn't fixed at compile time — use `nint.Size` instead in safe code.
